README_portal_auth
files.source:
/** 
 # QAcademy Portal — Auth v3 (PortalAuthv2)

Google Apps Script project providing **authentication and access control** for the QAcademy Blogger Portal (Project Alpha).

Handles:

- Email + password login
- Google Sign-In
- Self-signup (students) → creates account and redirects to login (NO auto-login)
- Products + subscriptions → course access
- Password reset (secure, token-based)
- Session tokens with device labels (with per-user session cap)
- Basic rate limiting + auth event logging
- Admin tools: create users, assign products, audit tokens/sessions, expiry reminders
- Time-driven cron: subscription expiry reminder emails

---

## 1. Project Structure

**Apps Script project name:** `PortalAuthv2`

Files:

1) `auth_config.gs`  
   - All configuration constants:
     - Spreadsheet ID
     - Sheet names (users, tokens, products, subscriptions, courses, auth_events, reset, etc.)
     - URLs (login page, dashboard, reset page, renew page, etc.)
     - Email template IDs (welcome, reset, product assigned, subscription expiring)
     - Brand name + social links
     - Token TTLs, reset TTL, etc.
     - **BUILD marker** (used by health endpoint)
   - **Rule:** constants only, no logic.

2) `auth_core_utils.gs`  
   - Low-level helpers with **no business meaning**:
     - Time/date helpers (`_now`, `_iso`, `_addDays`, `_addHours`, `_parseUtc`)
     - Sheets helpers (`_sh`, `_headers`, `_rows`, `_rowToObj`, `_appendObj`, `_updateObj`, `_findRowIndexBy`)
     - Booleans + parsing helpers (`_toBool_`, etc.)
     - Hashing + token/ID generation
   - **No HTTP, no JSON, no auth decisions.**

3) `auth_core_domain.gs`  
   - Core **auth/business logic**, independent of HTTP:
     - Auth event logging (`_logAuthEvent_`)
     - Login rate limiting (`_checkLoginRateLimit_`)
     - Email template rendering (Drive-based HTML)
     - Welcome emails, product assigned emails, subscription expiring emails
     - User lookup helpers (`_getUserByEmail`, `_getUserByUsername`, `_getUserById`, username generation)
     - Product / subscription / courses helpers:
       - `_getProductById`
       - `_getActiveSubscriptionsForUser`
       - `_getAccessFromSubscriptions`
       - Default trial assignment (`_ensureDefaultTrialSubscriptionForUser_`)
       - Subscription expiry reminders core (`_runExpiryRemindersCore_`)
     - Token primitives:
       - `_issueToken`, `_checkToken`, `_deactivateToken`, `_enforceTokenLimitForUser`
     - Password verification (`_verifyPassword`)
     - Google ID token verification (`_verifyGoogleIdToken_`)
   - **Rule:** no `doGet/doPost`, no direct `_json` responses.

4) `auth_actions_auth.gs`  
   - **Public-facing API + single router** (auth + admin actions are routed here):
     - `doGet(e)`:
       - `action=verify` (GET verify)
       - health/status (returns `{ ok:true, build, now }`)
     - `doPost(e)` routes to:
       - Auth:
         - `login` (legacy username)
         - `login_email`
         - `login_google`
         - `verify` (POST verify)
         - `logout`
         - `self_register` (public signup)
       - Password reset flow:
         - `reset_request`
         - `reset_check`
         - `reset_apply`
         - `reset_password` (token-based variant)
       - Admin:
         - `create_user`, `list_products`, `assign_product`
         - `list_tokens`, `admin_revoke_token`
         - `list_programs`, `admin_search_users`, `admin_get_user_subscriptions`
         - `send_expiry_reminders` (manual run)
         - `admin_list_auth_events`, `admin_list_reset_requests`
     - All these call core-domain helpers and return `_json(...)`.

   - Also includes cron entrypoint:
     - `cronSendExpiryReminders()` → calls `_runExpiryRemindersCore_()`
     - Intended for a time-driven Apps Script trigger.

5) `auth_actions_admin.gs`  
   - **Admin-only API endpoints** (called via `doPost(action=...)`):
     - `create_user` (admin-create user with temp password)
     - `list_products`
     - `assign_product` (creates subscription row)
     - `list_tokens` / `admin_revoke_token`
     - `list_programs`
     - `admin_search_users`
     - `admin_get_user_subscriptions`
     - `send_expiry_reminders` (manual trigger for expiry reminders)
     - Auth audit:
       - `admin_list_auth_events`
       - `admin_list_reset_requests`
   - **Rule:** light validation; heavy logic lives in core_domain.

6) `auth_test_helpers.gs`  
   - For manual checks & debugging only (non-production helpers).
   - Examples:
     - `TEST_authMail()` → approves MailApp scope
     - `TEST_verifyToken()` → server-side verify check
     - `ADMIN_setInitialPassword()` → one-off password setter (admin use only)
     - `TEST_grantExternalRequestScope()` → approves UrlFetch scope (Google Sign-In)
   - **Rule:** safe to disable in production; keep small and clearly labelled.

---

## 2. Data Model (Sheets Overview)

(All sheet names come from `auth_config.gs`.)

Typical key sheets:

- `SH_USERS`
  - `user_id`, `username`, `name`, `forename`, `surname`, `email`, `phone_number`
  - `program_id`, `cohort`, `avatar_url`
  - `salt`, `password_hash`, `must_change_password`
  - `role` (`STUDENT`, `ADMIN`, `MODERATOR`), `active`, `expires_utc`
  - `created_utc`, `last_login_utc`, `signup_source`

- `SH_TOKENS`
  - `token`, `user_id`
  - `kind` (`LOGIN`, `RESET`, etc.)
  - `issued_utc`, `expires_utc`, `last_seen_utc`
  - `ua_hash`, `ip_hash`, `device_label`, `login_via`
  - `active`

- `SH_PRODUCTS`
  - `product_id`, `name`, `description` (if present)
  - `kind` (`TRIAL`, `PAID`, `FREE`) (if present)
  - `status` (`ACTIVE`, etc.)
  - `duration_days`
  - `courses_included` (list used to derive dashboard access)

- `SH_SUBSCRIPTIONS`
  - `subscription_id`, `user_id`, `product_id`
  - `start_utc`, `expires_utc`
  - `status` (`ACTIVE`, etc.)
  - `expiry_reminded` (`''`, `'1'`, `'3'`, `'both'`) (used by reminders)

- `SH_COURSES`
  - `course_id`, `title`, `program_scope`, `status`, `page_slug`

- `SH_AUTH_EVENTS`
  - `event_id`, `ts_utc`, `kind` (`LOGIN_EMAIL`, `LOGIN_GOOGLE`, etc.)
  - `identifier` (email/username), `user_id`
  - `ip_hash`, `ua_hash`
  - `ok` (true/false)
  - `error_code`, `note`

- `SH_RESET`
  - `request_id`, `identifier_raw`, `email`, `username`
  - `requested_utc`, `status` (`REQUESTED`, `TOKEN_CREATED`, `EMAIL_SENT`, `CHECK_OK`, `USED`, `EXPIRED`, etc.)
  - `reset_token` (stored server-side; DO NOT expose in UIs)
  - `ip_hash`, `ua_hash`
  - `action_note`, `last_check_utc`, `used_utc` (if present)

---

## 3. Public API (Frontend Usage)

All calls hit the **same Web App URL**, using `action=...` via:
- `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` (preferred/simple), OR
- JSON body (optional support in router)

### Health / Status

- `GET` (no action)
  - Returns `{ ok:true, service:'QAcademyAuth', build:BUILD, now:... }`

### Verify (GET or POST)

- `GET ?action=verify&token=...`
- `POST action=verify` with `token`

Returns:
- `user` (basic profile)
- `access.courses` (course IDs derived from subscriptions/products)
- `access.expires_utc` (max subscription expiry)
- `flags.is_admin`
- `courses` (course metadata from `SH_COURSES`)

### Auth Actions

- `action=login`  
  - Legacy username + password login.

- `action=login_email`  
  - Email + password login.  
  - Returns `{ ok, token, token_expires_utc, profile }`.

- `action=login_google`  
  - Google Sign-In with `id_token`.  
  - Verifies via Google tokeninfo endpoint and matches `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`.
  - Returns `{ ok, token, token_expires_utc, profile }`.

- `action=logout`  
  - Deactivates the token.

- `action=self_register`  
  - Public signup: forename, surname, email, password (+ optional program/cohort/phone/avatar/username).
  - Creates `STUDENT`, assigns default trial product (`WELCOME_TRIAL`), sends welcome email.
  - **Does NOT auto-login.** Returns `{ ok:true, redirect: LOGIN_PAGE_URL?... }`.

---

## 4. Password Reset Flow (Public)

- `action=reset_request`  
  - Input: `email` or `username` (identifier).
  - Logs request, rate-limits, issues RESET token, sends reset email containing link:
    - `RESET_PAGE_URL?rid=...&rt=...`
  - Always returns generic success message (no account existence leak).

- `action=reset_check`  
  - Input: `request_id`, `reset_token`.
  - Returns `{ ok, status: 'valid' | 'expired' | 'invalid' }` for the front-end.

- `action=reset_apply`  
  - Input: `request_id`, `reset_token`, `new_password`.
  - Updates user password, marks reset request USED, deactivates the reset token.

- `action=reset_password`  
  - Token-based reset variant (optional/future): `token` + `password`.

---

## 5. Admin API (Back-office Tools)

All admin actions require a valid admin token.

- `create_user`  
  - Admin/moderator-created user, temp password, optional role/program/cohort.
  - Sends welcome email with login details.
  - Notes:
    - ADMIN can create ADMIN/MODERATOR
    - MODERATOR can only create STUDENT

- `assign_product`  
  - Assigns a product to a user (by `user_id` or `email`), creating a subscription.

- `list_products`  
  - Lists products for admin UIs.

- `list_tokens` / `admin_revoke_token`  
  - Token/session audit & control.

- `list_programs`  
  - For dropdowns when creating users.

- `admin_search_users`  
  - Search users by email/name/ID.

- `admin_get_user_subscriptions`  
  - Show all subscriptions for a specific user.

- `send_expiry_reminders`  
  - Manually triggers reminder scan (`_runExpiryRemindersCore_`).

- `admin_list_auth_events`  
  - Lists login attempts/events with filters (user_id, email, kind, time range, etc.).
  - Privacy: does NOT expose IP/UA hashes.

- `admin_list_reset_requests`  
  - Lists password reset requests with filters/status.
  - Security: does NOT expose reset_token.

---

## 6. Flows (High Level)

### Login (email / username / Google)

1) Frontend sends `action=login_email` / `login` / `login_google`.
2) Rate limit checked via `_checkLoginRateLimit_`.
3) User loaded; active + expiry checked.
4) Per-user session cap enforced (`_enforceTokenLimitForUser`).
5) New token issued via `_issueToken` with `device_label` + `login_via`.
6) Response includes token + profile.
7) Auth event written to `SH_AUTH_EVENTS` with outcome + code.

### Self-Registration (Public signup)

1) Frontend sends `action=self_register`.
2) Email uniqueness checked; user row created with salt+hash password.
3) Default trial subscription assigned via `_ensureDefaultTrialSubscriptionForUser_(..., 'WELCOME_TRIAL')`.
4) Welcome email sent.
5) **No token issued.** Returns redirect URL to login page.

### Password Reset

1) `reset_request`: user enters email/username → generic success response.
2) Email contains link `RESET_PAGE_URL?rid=<request_id>&rt=<reset_token>`.
3) `reset_check` validates link/token status for the reset page UI.
4) `reset_apply` sets new password, marks request used, deactivates reset token.

### Products & Subscriptions → Access

- `apiVerify`:
  1) Validates token.
  2) Loads user; ensures active + not expired.
  3) Loads ACTIVE, in-range subscriptions.
  4) Derives course IDs from products’ `courses_included`.
  5) Returns course list + overall expiry.

### Expiry Reminder Emails (Cron + Manual)

- **Cron:** time-driven trigger calls `cronSendExpiryReminders()` → `_runExpiryRemindersCore_()`.
- **Manual:** admin calls `action=send_expiry_reminders`.

Typical behaviour:
- Scans ACTIVE subscriptions.
- Computes days left.
- Sends reminders for target day windows (e.g. 3 days and 1 day remaining).
- Updates `expiry_reminded` to avoid duplicate sends.
- Returns stats (`scanned`, `sent`, `sentByDay`) and logs via `Logger`.

---

## 7. Deployment & Permissions

### Deployment (Web App)

Deploy as **Web App**:
- Execute as: **Me (script owner)**
- Who has access: choose based on your portal design (token gating still applies)

### First-run permissions

Before relying on production flows/triggers, run these once in the editor:
- `TEST_authMail()` → approves `MailApp.sendEmail`
- `TEST_grantExternalRequestScope()` → approves `UrlFetchApp.fetch` (Google Sign-In verification)

Then run once manually:
- `cronSendExpiryReminders()` (so trigger runs won’t fail due to missing scopes)

### Time-driven trigger

Create a time-driven trigger for:
- Function: `cronSendExpiryReminders`
- Frequency: daily (or twice daily if you want faster retries)
- Timezone: set Script timezone to **Europe/London** (Project Settings)

---

## 8. Security Notes

- `SH_RESET` stores `reset_token` server-side for validation.
  - Treat this sheet as sensitive; restrict editors.
  - Admin listing endpoints must never expose reset tokens (current code complies).

- Tokens are capped per user (`_enforceTokenLimitForUser(user_id, 2)` in login flows).
  - This helps reduce account sharing and session sprawl.

- Auth audit endpoints intentionally avoid exposing IP/UA hashes to admin UIs.

---

## 9. Development Rules (Where to Put New Code)

- **New config value?**  
  → `auth_config.gs`

- **Pure helper (date, sheet I/O, hashing, parsing)?**  
  → `auth_core_utils.gs`

- **Business rule / domain logic (users, tokens, products, subscriptions, emails, rate limits)?**  
  → `auth_core_domain.gs`

- **New public endpoint (used by login/signup/reset pages, portal UI)?**  
  → Route + thin wrapper in `auth_actions_auth.gs`, heavy logic in `auth_core_domain.gs`.

- **New admin endpoint (used by admin dashboard/tools)?**  
  → Route + thin wrapper in `auth_actions_auth.gs` + handler in `auth_actions_admin.gs`, heavy logic in `auth_core_domain.gs`.

- **One-off test/diagnostic helper?**  
  → `auth_test_helpers.gs`

Keeping this separation will make cloning to PROD and future upgrades much easier.
*/
